questions.question: Evaluate \(\int \frac{\cos x}{\sqrt{1 + \sin^2 x}} \, dx\)
questions.explanation: Let \(u = \sin x\), \(du = \cos x dx\). Then \(\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{1+u^2}} du = \ln|u + \sqrt{1+u^2}| + C = \ln|\sin x + \sqrt{1 + \sin^2 x}| + C\).

questions.question: Evaluate \(\int x^2 \cdot \sqrt{1 - x^6} \, dx\)
questions.explanation: Let \(u = x^3\), \(du = 3x^2 dx\) so \(x^2 dx = \frac{du}{3}\). Then \(\frac{1}{3} \int \sqrt{1-u^2} du = \frac{1}{3} \cdot \frac{1}{2} (u\sqrt{1-u^2} + \arcsin u) + C = \frac{1}{6}(x^3\sqrt{1-x^6} + \arcsin(x^3)) + C\).

questions.question: Evaluate \(\int \frac{\sqrt{x}}{1 + x^3} \, dx\)
questions.explanation: Let \(u = x^{3/2}\), then \(u^2 = x^3\), \(du = \frac{3}{2} x^{1/2} dx\) so \(\sqrt{x} dx = \frac{2}{3} du\). Then \(\int \frac{1}{1+u^2} \cdot \frac{2}{3} du = \frac{2}{3} \arctan u + C = \frac{2}{3} \arctan(x^{3/2}) + C\).

questions.question: Evaluate \(\int \frac{e^x}{\sqrt{e^{2x} + 1}} \, dx\)
questions.explanation: Let \(u = e^x\), \(du = e^x dx\). Then \(\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{u^2+1}} du = \ln|u + \sqrt{u^2+1}| + C = \ln|e^x + \sqrt{e^{2x}+1}| + C\).

questions.question: Evaluate \(\int \frac{1}{x \sqrt{1 - (\ln x)^2}} \, dx\)
questions.explanation: Let \(u = \ln x\), \(du = \frac{1}{x} dx\). Then \(\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{1-u^2}} du = \arcsin u + C = \arcsin(\ln x) + C\).

questions.question: Evaluate \(\int \tan^3 x \cdot \sec x \, dx\)
questions.explanation: Let \(u = \sec x\), \(du = \sec x \tan x dx\). Note \(\tan^3 x \sec x dx = \tan^2 x \cdot (\sec x \tan x dx) = (u^2 - 1) du\). Then \(\int (u^2 - 1) du = \frac{u^3}{3} - u + C = \frac{\sec^3 x}{3} - \sec x + C\).

questions.question: Evaluate \(\int \frac{x^2 - 1}{(x^2 + 1) \sqrt{x^4 + 1}} \, dx\)
questions.explanation: Divide numerator and denominator by \(x^2\): \(\int \frac{1 - 1/x^2}{(x + 1/x) \sqrt{x^2 + 1/x^2}} dx\). Let \(u = x + 1/x\), \(du = (1 - 1/x^2)dx\). Note \(x^2 + 1/x^2 = u^2 - 2\). Then \(\int \frac{du}{u \sqrt{u^2 - 2}} = \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \arcsec\left(\frac{u}{\sqrt{2}}\right) + C = \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \arcsec\left(\frac{x + 1/x}{\sqrt{2}}\right) + C\).

questions.question: Evaluate \(\int \frac{\sin x \cos x}{\sin^4 x + \cos^4 x} \, dx\)
questions.explanation: Let \(u = \sin^2 x\), \(du = 2\sin x \cos x dx\) so \(\sin x \cos x dx = \frac{du}{2}\). Note \(\sin^4 x + \cos^4 x = u^2 + (1-u)^2 = 2u^2 - 2u + 1 = \frac{1}{2}[4(u-\frac{1}{2})^2 + 1]\). After simplification and substitution \(w = 2(u-\frac{1}{2})\), the integral becomes \(\frac{1}{2} \arctan w + C = \frac{1}{2} \arctan(2\sin^2 x - 1) + C = \frac{1}{2} \arctan(-\cos 2x) + C = -\frac{1}{2} \arctan(\cos 2x) + C\).

scoreMessages.90: Exceptional! You've conquered the most challenging u-substitution problems!
scoreMessages.70: Excellent work! You're proficient with complex substitutions. Review the challenging ones.
scoreMessages.50: Good effort! These are tough problems. Keep practicing multi-step substitutions.
scoreMessages.0: Review advanced substitution techniques including trigonometric substitution and algebraic manipulation.
